description: OpenSSH versions before 7.4 are affected by an agent-forwarding vulnerability that can allow arbitrary library loading.
recommendation: Upgrade OpenSSH to the latest stable release.

description: Older OpenSSH releases contain multiple memory-safety issues in code paths reachable pre-authentication.
recommendation: Upgrade OpenSSH to the latest stable release.

description: Path traversal and remote code execution in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.49 via crafted request paths.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTP Server to 2.4.51 or later immediately.

description: Path traversal and RCE in Apache 2.4.50 due to an incomplete fix for CVE-2021-41773.
recommendation: Upgrade Apache HTTP Server to 2.4.52 or later.

description: DNS resolver off-by-one heap write in nginx versions before 1.20.1 / 1.21.0.
recommendation: Upgrade nginx and avoid using the resolver directive with untrusted input.

description: vsftpd 2.3.4 contains a backdoor that grants a remote root shell on port 6200 when a specific login string is used.
recommendation: Upgrade vsftpd immediately and verify the binary against a trusted source.

description: ProFTPD's mod_copy allows unauthenticated remote attackers to copy files, leading to remote code execution.
recommendation: Upgrade ProFTPD and disable mod_copy if not required.

description: Buffer overflow in the WebDAV service of IIS 6.0 allows remote code execution.
recommendation: Upgrade to a supported IIS version and disable WebDAV if unused.

description: MySQL before 5.7.15 is vulnerable to configuration-file injection leading to arbitrary code execution.
recommendation: Upgrade MySQL to a supported, patched release.

description: Exim before 4.92 is vulnerable to remote command execution via crafted RCPT TO addresses.
